philosophy.mental_model: Encode human pattern recognition as explicit rules
philosophy.inductive_bias: Assumes patterns are consistent and enumerable
philosophy.strengths: High precision, no training data needed, fully interpretable, zero latency addition
philosophy.weaknesses: Low recall on edge cases, requires manual maintenance, brittle to format changes
philosophy.best_for: Stable, well-defined formats; high-precision requirements; no ML infrastructure

philosophy.mental_model: Leverage large language model reasoning for zero/few-shot extraction
philosophy.inductive_bias: Natural language understanding generalizes to structured extraction
philosophy.strengths: No training needed, handles novel formats, strong reasoning, easy to update
philosophy.weaknesses: High latency, API costs, potential hallucination, rate limits, non-deterministic
philosophy.best_for: Low volume, complex documents, rapid prototyping, schema flexibility

philosophy.mental_model: Transfer knowledge from massive pretraining to specific task
philosophy.inductive_bias: Language understanding from pretraining generalizes to extraction
philosophy.strengths: Strong performance with little data, captures semantics, state-of-the-art
philosophy.weaknesses: Large model size, slow inference, may overfit on small data
philosophy.best_for: When accuracy is paramount, sufficient compute available
